text.title: Sidekiq Background Jobs for Rails Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every Sidekiq Background Jobs for Rails application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every `perform` MUST be idempotent: re-running with the same args has no extra side effect.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Sidekiq best-practices docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Pass IDs and primitive values to `perform_async`; never serialize ActiveRecord objects.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Sidekiq best-practices docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Declare `sidekiq_options retry: N, dead: true` per job — never rely on global defaults.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Sidekiq docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Implement `sidekiq_retry_in` to widen backoff for known transient errors (HTTP 5xx, timeouts).
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Sidekiq docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Catch `ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound` — the record may have been deleted between enqueue and run.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Sidekiq best-practices docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Job class only enqueues + delegates; business logic lives in the service object.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Internal Rails review patterns. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Pin concurrency per queue (default 5 — critical 10 — mailers 2); never share one pool.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Sidekiq Enterprise docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold and no duplicate exists.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Update the existing artefact in place (bump version, refresh last_reviewed).
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when a duplicate is found.
